name: Parkside Financial Bank & Trust
description_short: Parkside Financial Bank & Trust is an FDIC-insured bank offering commercial banking, family office services, trust administration, and 401(k) plan services integrated within a single institution.
description_long:
Parkside Financial Bank & Trust operates as a full-service bank with FDIC insurance backing, positioning itself as a specialized financial partner for high-net-worth individuals and businesses. The company describes itself as offering an 'uncommon partnership' model that combines traditional banking with wealth management and retirement plan administration under one roof. Founded on the principle of collaborative client relationships, the bank emphasizes strategic, efficient solutions tailored to complex financial needs.

The institution provides a comprehensive suite of services spanning three primary divisions: Commercial Banking (including commercial lending, deposit services, private equity and sponsor finance, search fund financing, and treasury management); Family Office Services (investment management, financial reporting, tax mitigation, risk management, retirement planning, and estate/philanthropic planning); and Trust Services (trustee services, investment management, specialty asset management, custody, estate settlement, and special needs trusts). Additionally, the bank operates a 401(k) plan services division offering 3(38) investment manager services, custom plan design, investments and due diligence, fee transparency, and client education.

Parkside distinguishes itself through its integrated business model that consolidates services typically fragmented across multiple providers. Rather than focusing on retail banking products, the company explicitly targets clients who have built significant wealth and require sophisticated planning services. The bank markets its expertise in family transitions, business transformations, and wealth preservation, emphasizing collaboration between client teams and in-house specialists across estate planning, retirement planning, tax, and insurance domains.

The company appears to be a private bank serving high-net-worth clients and businesses rather than a consumer-focused retail institution. Website content emphasizes professional advisory relationships and complex financial planning rather than basic checking/savings accounts. Limited information is available regarding specific account types, deposit rates, loan terms, or retail banking products. The organization's accessibility appears limited to clients meeting minimum relationship requirements typical of private banking institutions.
services: Estate settlement and special needs trusts
401(k) plan administration and 3(38) investment manager services
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full backing of U.S. Government
Integrated business model combining commercial banking, trust services, and 401(k) administration under one institution
Comprehensive family office services including investment management, tax mitigation, estate planning, and risk management
Specialized expertise in private equity and sponsor finance, search fund financing, and complex commercial lending
Dedicated advisory teams with in-house specialists in estate planning, retirement planning, tax, and insurance
Multiple custody and trust administration capabilities including special needs trusts and administrative trustee services
Digital access through mobile app (available on Google Play and App Store) and online banking portal
cons: Website provides no information about minimum account balances or relationship requirements, suggesting this is a private bank not accessible to typical consumers
No retail banking product details (checking/savings rates, fees, minimum deposits) are disclosed on public website
Limited transparency regarding fee structures despite marketing 'fee transparency' as a service offering
Website does not specify geographic service areas or branch locations for in-person banking
Appears to serve only high-net-worth individuals and institutional clients, not suitable for average consumer banking needs
best_for: High-net-worth individuals and families requiring comprehensive wealth management and estate planning services
Business owners and investors needing integrated commercial banking with private equity and sponsor finance expertise
Employers seeking institutional 401(k) plan administration with professional investment management and compliance support
Families requiring trust administration, custodial services, and multi-generational wealth transition planning
diagnosis: Parkside Financial Bank & Trust is designed for high-net-worth individuals, business owners, and institutional clients requiring sophisticated, integrated wealth management and commercial banking services—not for consumers seeking standard retail banking products. The primary caveat is that this is a private bank with undisclosed account minimums and relationship requirements that likely makes it inaccessible to typical consumers looking for basic checking, savings, or consumer loan products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
